description_long: This object is a vintage-style arcade game cabinet with a robust, rectangular frame and a slightly angled front panel. The exterior is primarily a worn, metallic blue with visible rust and weathering, suggesting long-term use or exposure to the elements. The front features a recessed control panel equipped with two joysticks and multiple colored buttons, indicating support for two players. Above the control panel is a large, dark display area, likely for a screen, with a wide, unadorned marquee section at the top. The side panels are smooth and unembellished, lacking any decorative graphics or branding. The back of the cabinet is solid and featureless, reinforcing its utilitarian design.
description: A weathered, metallic blue arcade game cabinet with a rusted finish, dual joysticks, and multiple colored buttons on a recessed control panel. The cabinet has a plain marquee and unadorned side panels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cabinet's depth, angled front, and smooth, unadorned blue panel with visible wear.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the control panel with two joysticks, multiple colored buttons, a large screen area, and a plain marquee.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, highlighting the cabinet's profile and the protruding control panel.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a solid, featureless blue back panel with no visible access points or graphics.
attribute_control_panel_layout: Two joysticks and multiple colored buttons arranged for two-player side-by-side play; no visible trackballs or spinners.
